preamble: Nasal Lowering involves the shifting of a nasalized vowel to a lower tongue position. This process often maximizes the acoustic space for nasal resonance, which is clearer in lower, more open vowels.

examples.note: Historical lowering of nasalized /e/ in French (Latin dentem > Old French dẽnt > Modern French dɑ̃).

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
